findings.description: NCDENR logo/seal image missing from HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF shows NCDENR seal/logo at top of page. HTML has no image, just text 'NCDENR'.

findings.description: Handwritten initials/signatures not preserved in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows handwritten initials next to FROM field (Karen Higgins and Brian Wrenn signatures). HTML has plain text only.

findings.description: Header layout differs - PDF has three-column header with Governor/Director/Secretary, HTML has similar but slightly different spacing
findings.evidence: PDF has Beverly Eaves Perdue, Charles Wakild P.E., and Dee Freeman in a three-column layout. HTML preserves this but with different alignment.

findings.description: One North Carolina Naturally logo missing from HTML footer
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'One NorthCarolina Naturally' logo at bottom right of page 1. HTML renders this as text.

findings.description: Table formatting differs but data is preserved
findings.evidence: PDF shows Corps' Regional General Permit Number and DWQ General Certification Number as a formatted table with underlined headers. HTML renders as a proper HTML table with different styling.
PDF has underlined section headings (I. and II.), HTML uses larger heading styles without underline.
PDF has footer with address/phone/fax at bottom left. HTML consolidates this differently.

findings.description: The h1 is 'NCDENR' which is derived from the letterhead logo text, not the document title. The actual document subject is in the RE: line. This is acceptable for a conversion but worth noting.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

NCDENR

. The document is a memo with RE: subject line about UMBI Template and WQC/IWP revisions.

findings.description: There is a nested <main> inside <main>, creating invalid HTML structure, though this does not affect content fidelity.
All seven images have generic alt text 'Scanned document page X' which is not descriptive of actual content but is not fabricated.
findings.evidence: All img tags use alt='Scanned document page 1' through 'Scanned document page 7'

findings.description: The PDF shows the last list item 'i' in the thresholds list using lowercase Roman numeral style, but the HTML switches to list-style-type: lower-roman mid-list, which is inconsistent with the ordered list type='a' used for the other items.
findings.evidence: PDF shows items a-h then i. HTML uses <li style='list-style-type: lower-roman;'> for item i within an <ol type='a'>.

findings.description: The entire scanned page images are included alongside the fully transcribed text content, effectively duplicating all content - once as images and once as text. While this may be intentional for the conversion format, it represents significant duplication.
findings.evidence: Seven

findings.description: Minor text normalization: PDF shows 'allthe' (run together) on page 1 and 'allthe' appears corrected to 'all the' in HTML. Also 'UMBltemplate' in PDF corrected to 'UMBI template'. These are acceptable corrections of OCR artifacts.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1: 'allthe parties involved' and 'UMBltemplate'; HTML: 'all the parties involved' and 'UMBI template'
